notes: Snapdragon 8 Gen 2. Settings.System keys verified on Odin 3 hardware (same firmware namespace); Odin 2 confirmation pending. fan_mode + performance_mode are int values flipped by AYN's own Quick Settings tile.

communityPresets.name: Underclock — Large
communityPresets.description: TheOldTaylor's aggressive cap. ~14W from ~20W in 1080p AAA, temps 50-60C from 90-100C. Reported by author; safety not formally verified.

communityPresets.name: Underclock — Medium
communityPresets.description: TheOldTaylor's middle preset. Less power savings than Large but more headroom for AAA.

communityPresets.name: Underclock — Small
communityPresets.description: TheOldTaylor's mildest underclock. Small thermal/power benefit, almost no performance loss.

notes: Snapdragon 8 Elite (Dragonwing). 2-cluster: policy0 (6 cores, stock max 3532.8 MHz) + policy6 (2 prime, stock max 4320 MHz). NOT 8 Gen 2 topology — no policy4/7. Three perf daemons must stop+chmod-lock around cpufreq writes or they race the value back; see TheOldTaylor scripts.

Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 (QCS8550 / kalama), Android 13. THREE CPU clusters: policy0 (cores 0-2, little, max 2016 MHz), policy3 (cores 3-6, gold, max 2803 MHz), policy7 (core 7, prime, max 3187 MHz). GPU Adreno 740 (max 680 MHz). fan_mode Settings.System key VERIFIED present on Thor firmware (value observed: 4). performance_mode key NOT present on Thor (Odin 3 has it, Thor does not) — perf preset switching goes through com.qualcomm.qti.performancemode / Game Assistant instead, so perfPresetAdapter is null here. scaling_max_freq is mode 660 owned system:system — no direct UID write; live tuning needs root or the one-shot Odin runner script.
Pocket 5 is passively cooled. Sysfs discovery for clocking only.

Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 (QCS8550 / kalama, mfr Moorechip), Android 13. SAME 3-cluster topology as AYN Thor: policy0 (cores 0-2, little, 2016 MHz), policy3 (cores 3-6, gold, 2803 MHz), policy7 (core 7, prime, 3187 MHz). GPU Adreno 740 (max 680 MHz). Storage YMTC YMUS9B4TF2D1C1 (UFS 4.0). FAN (ZERO-SETUP, no root): a normal app binds the registered 'SettingsController' service via ServiceManager, asks it for the 'FanProvider' binder (txn 1, token com.ro.settings.IExternalControlManager), then drives the fan on FanProvider (token com.ro.settings.IFanControlProvider): txn 5 c(int)=set mode, txn 7 r(int)=set CUSTOM fan SPEED (a single scalar on a ~25000 duty/RPM scale, NOT a temp curve and NOT 0-255 PWM), txn 2 b()=read current value (readback-verify, returned 25000). VERIFIED on-device (RP6 13f60c13): bind + txn 7 accepted + txn 2 read 25000. This is a custom held SPEED, not a temperature-reactive curve like Odin/AYANEO. The exact CUSTOM-mode int + working speed range are calibrated live (RetroidFanConfig). Vendor app com.rp.gameassistant owns the legacy fan_mode Settings.System key (value 4) but the FanProvider binder is the real control path. scaling_max_freq is mode 660 owned system:system — per-file SELinux denial observed (policy0 max denied, policy3/7 allowed) so monitor falls back to OPP bounds; live cpufreq tuning needs root or a one-shot script.

Snapdragon G3x Gen 2 (SG8275, QTI), Android 13. SELinux Permissive but DAC perms still deny cpufreq writes to the app UID — scaling_max_freq is system:system mode 660. THREE CPU clusters: policy0 (little), policy3 (gold/prime-by-caps), policy7 (top OPP) — same topology family as AYN Thor / RP6. Prime cluster determined at runtime by maxByOrNull on available freqs. GPU Adreno (kgsl-3d0, devfreq present, 124.8–680 MHz). ZERO-SETUP LIVE PATH (0.1.31, verified on-device): com.ayaneo.gamewindow exports an unguarded AIDL service (.utils.aidl.AyaAidlService); a normal app binds it and sends 'clientId:msg_type_performance:com_set_performance_*' commands, and the overlay (uid=system) does the privileged sysfs write. No root, no Shizuku, no script. AutoTDP resolves LIVE via AyaneoVendorWriter when this binder is reachable. CUSTOM FAN CURVES are now driven via this SAME gamewindow binder (zero-setup, no config.xml): send 'com_set_fan_speed_strategy:FAN_MODE_CUSTOM-temp,duty|temp,duty|…' (duty is a 0-100 %) + 'com_set_fan_speed_is_linear:LINEAR|STEP'; verify by reading the app-readable hwmon pwm1 node (/sys/devices/platform/soc/soc:pwm-fan/hwmon/hwmon0/pwm1, 0-255 → pwm/255*100 %). The curve still passes Calibrate's hard hot-zone cooling floor (≥45%@95C, ≥60%@105C) before it can apply — an AYANEO curve cannot overheat the device. The fanAdapter SERVICE_INTENT target is the bound AIDL service (NOT a broadcast). Settings.System fan_mode/performance_mode keys are INERT here (writing them does nothing — gated by 0.1.30 readback-verify). com.aya.gsset is a Google android_id spoofer, not perf. If the gamewindow binder is unreachable (firmware variant), AutoTDP AND custom fan curves degrade to unavailable honestly; Shizuku/root remain optional fallbacks for tuning.
Fan is controlled by AYASpace via private binder. SERVICE_INTENT action is a placeholder pending reverse-engineering.
Helio G99, passive. Sysfs clocking only.
